title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games
summary: Kaspersky researchers analyze new Argamal RAT distributed via infected hentai games and allowing the attacker to control the target machine.

title: Wardriving assessment across Mexico: Preparing for the 2026 World Cup
summary: In the lead-up to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Kaspersky GReAT experts conducted a wardriving assessment in Mexico City, Monterrey, and Guadalajara to evaluate Wi-Fi hotspot security configurations and potential exposure risks.

title: Containers on fire: from container escapes to supply chain attacks
summary: We break down the primary attack vectors in containerized environments: exposed secrets, privilege misconfigurations, API compromise, and supply chain attacks.

title: What’s in the container? Analyzing vulnerabilities, risks and protection with Kaspersky Container Security and the KIRA AI assistant
summary: What are the main risks for container environments: vulnerabilities, supply chain attacks, configuration errors; how to improve container security and how Kaspersky Container Security with the KIRA AI assistant can help.

title: Pirates in the crosshairs: how one cybercrime gang has been infecting book, movie, and TV show fans for years
summary: Our experts continue to track attacks targeting consumers of pirated content, both books and movies. 2026 saw the discovery of new target sites with tens of millions of visitors, while the miner gained a RAT module.

title: Cloud Atlas activity in the second half of 2025 and early 2026: new tools and a new payload
summary: Cloud Atlas attacks the public sector and diplomatic structures of Russia and Belarus, using ReverseSocks, SSH, and Tor for persistence in infected systems and its new tool, PowerCloud.

title: How an image could compromise your Mac: understanding an ExifTool vulnerability (CVE-2026-3102)
summary: We explain how a flaw in ExifTool allows attackers to compromise macOS systems via a malicious image (CVE-2026-3102).

title: IT threat evolution in Q1 2026. Mobile statistics
summary: This report contains mobile threat statistics for Q1 2026, along with noteworthy discoveries and quarterly trends: new versions of SparkCat and Triada.

title: IT threat evolution in Q1 2026. Non-mobile statistics
summary: The report presents key trends and statistics on malware that targeted personal computers running Windows and macOS, as well as Internet of Things (IoT) devices, during Q1 2026.

title: Kimsuky targets organizations with PebbleDash-based tools
summary: Kaspersky researchers analyze a range of new PebbleDash-based tools used in recent Kimsuky campaigns and reveal their connection to the AppleSeed malware cluster.
